title: vitis shuttleworthii
intro: Vitis shuttleworthii, commonly known as the wild grapevine, is a plant species native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been found to possess several health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The therapeutic actions of Vitis shuttleworthii include antimicrobial and antiviral effects, as well as the potential to modulate the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include polyphenols, flavonoids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
